verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 encodes TGN46, a type I single-pass TM protein canonically resident in the trans-Golgi network. It continuously cycles between the TGN and the plasma membrane via clathrin-mediated endocytosis, with a small but documented fraction present at the cell surface at any steady-state time point. Surface biotinylation and antibody-uptake studies have confirmed a minor PM pool before rapid internalization. This constitutive TGN-PM cycling places it in dual_localization: it reaches the outer leaflet by its own TM anchor, but its dominant steady-state compartment is the TGN, not the PM.
